sections.paragraph:
πανούργως adv. of πανοῦργος (Aristoph., Equ. 317; Pla., Soph. 239c et al.; Sb 8026, 14; Ps 82:4 Sym.; Philo, Poster. Cai. 82) deceitfully πάντοτε πανούργως ἔζησα μετὰ πάντων Hm 3:3 (on the wordplay s. B-D-F §488, 1; Rob. 1201).—TW.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 754
